terms.definition: A patient's voluntary agreement to a treatment after being given clear information about its purpose, risks, benefits and alternatives.
terms.exampleSentence: I obtained informed consent for the cannulation and documented it in the notes.
terms.contextNotes: Capacity must be assumed unless proven otherwise (MCA 2005).

terms.definition: The ability to understand, retain, weigh up and communicate a specific decision at a specific time, under the Mental Capacity Act 2005.
terms.exampleSentence: She has mental capacity to refuse the antibiotics, even though we disagree with her decision.
terms.contextNotes: Decision-specific and time-specific; not all-or-nothing.

terms.definition: A decision made on behalf of an adult lacking capacity, considering their wishes, feelings, beliefs and values per MCA 2005.
terms.exampleSentence: We held a best interests meeting before agreeing to the PEG insertion.
terms.contextNotes: Family is consulted but does not 'consent' under MCA.

terms.definition: A structured, compassionate communication of information that adversely affects a patient's view of their future.
terms.exampleSentence: Use the SPIKES framework when breaking bad news about the diagnosis.
terms.contextNotes: Use a quiet room; allow silence; avoid jargon.

terms.definition: Active holistic care of patients with advanced progressive illness, focused on quality of life and symptom control.
terms.exampleSentence: We have referred her to the palliative care team for symptom control.
terms.contextNotes: Not synonymous with end-of-life — can begin from diagnosis.

terms.definition: Care provided in the last days of life, focused on comfort, dignity and supporting patient and family wishes.
terms.exampleSentence: She has been recognised as end-of-life and we have started anticipatory medications.
terms.contextNotes: Replaced the Liverpool Care Pathway after 2014.

terms.definition: A small portable pump delivering subcutaneous medication continuously over 24 hours, often used in palliative care.
terms.exampleSentence: Set up a syringe driver with morphine and midazolam over 24 hours.
terms.contextNotes: Site checked at least 4-hourly for inflammation or precipitation.

terms.definition: Pre-prescribed PRN drugs (typically opioid, antiemetic, anxiolytic, antisecretory) ready for symptom relief in end-of-life care.
terms.exampleSentence: Anticipatory medication is prescribed in case of pain, agitation or secretions overnight.
terms.contextNotes: Standardised four drugs in NHS community palliative care.

terms.definition: Nursing care focused on relieving symptoms and providing dignity rather than curative treatment.
terms.exampleSentence: We have moved to comfort care only and stopped the antibiotics.
terms.contextNotes: Should be communicated clearly to family and team.

terms.definition: Infection of any part of the urinary system, presenting with dysuria, frequency, urgency and sometimes confusion in elderly patients.
terms.exampleSentence: Send a midstream urine sample — she may have a urinary tract infection.
terms.contextNotes: New confusion in elderly may be the only sign of UTI.

terms.definition: An abrupt deterioration in renal function defined by rising creatinine or falling urine output, staged 1-3.
terms.exampleSentence: His acute kidney injury is stage 2 — we have stopped the ACE inhibitor.
terms.contextNotes: Sick day rules: hold ACEi, ARB, NSAIDs, metformin and diuretics.

terms.definition: Infection involving the airways below the larynx, often presenting with productive cough, fever and crackles.
terms.exampleSentence: Treat the lower respiratory tract infection with amoxicillin per local guidance.
terms.contextNotes: Pneumonia is a specific severe form of LRTI.

terms.definition: Life-threatening organ dysfunction caused by a dysregulated host response to infection; requires Sepsis Six within one hour.
terms.exampleSentence: Suspected sepsis — initiate the Sepsis Six bundle now.
terms.contextNotes: UK Sepsis Trust promotes the Sepsis Six bundle.

terms.definition: Six interventions delivered within one hour of suspected sepsis: oxygen, blood cultures, IV antibiotics, IV fluids, lactate, urine output.
terms.exampleSentence: We have completed the Sepsis Six within 45 minutes of recognition.
terms.contextNotes: Often described as 'Give 3, Take 3'.

terms.definition: An acute, fluctuating disturbance of consciousness, attention and cognition, often triggered by infection, drugs or pain.
terms.exampleSentence: Her delirium is likely secondary to UTI — review her medications and rehydrate.
terms.contextNotes: 4AT is the NHS-preferred bedside screen for delirium.

terms.definition: A four-item bedside tool screening for delirium and cognitive impairment in older inpatients.
terms.exampleSentence: Her 4AT score is 5, suggesting probable delirium.
terms.contextNotes: Score ≥4 suggests possible delirium and/or cognitive impairment.

terms.definition: Temporary cessation of bowel motility after surgery, presenting with abdominal distension, vomiting and absent bowel sounds.
terms.exampleSentence: Likely post-operative ileus — keep nil by mouth and consider an NG tube.
terms.contextNotes: Distinguish from mechanical obstruction radiologically.

terms.definition: A blood clot, usually in the deep veins of the leg, presenting with unilateral calf swelling, warmth and pain.
terms.exampleSentence: Her left calf is swollen and tender — suspected deep vein thrombosis.
terms.contextNotes: Inpatient VTE assessment is mandated within 14h of admission.

terms.definition: Obstruction of the pulmonary arterial circulation by a thrombus, presenting with breathlessness, pleuritic pain and tachycardia.
terms.exampleSentence: Sudden desaturation and tachycardia — consider pulmonary embolism.
terms.contextNotes: Confirmed on CT pulmonary angiogram (CTPA).

terms.definition: Separation of the layers of a surgical wound after closure, partially or completely.
terms.exampleSentence: There is partial wound dehiscence of the lower abdominal incision.
terms.contextNotes: Cover with saline-soaked gauze; escalate to surgical team.

terms.definition: Infection occurring at or near a surgical incision within 30 days of surgery (or 90 days with implants).
terms.exampleSentence: The wound is erythematous with purulent discharge — likely surgical site infection.
terms.contextNotes: Categorised superficial, deep or organ/space.

terms.definition: Infrequent or difficult defaecation, often with hard stool, straining and a sense of incomplete evacuation.
terms.exampleSentence: She has had no bowel motion for five days — please review for constipation.
terms.contextNotes: Co-prescribe a laxative with regular opioids.

terms.definition: An unintentional descent to ground level from a standing position, an in-hospital safety event requiring incident reporting.
terms.exampleSentence: Witnessed fall from a standing height — please complete neurological observations and Datix.
terms.contextNotes: Always perform post-fall NEWS2 and neuro obs if anticoagulated.

terms.definition: A formal notification to the local authority safeguarding team when an adult or child is at risk of abuse or neglect.
terms.exampleSentence: I am making a safeguarding referral due to suspected financial abuse by the carer.
terms.contextNotes: Care Act 2014 underpins adult safeguarding in England.

terms.definition: The proactive multidisciplinary process of preparing a patient for safe discharge, including equipment, follow-up and social support.
terms.exampleSentence: We started discharge planning on day one to avoid delayed transfer of care.
terms.contextNotes: Aim to avoid delayed transfer of care (DTOC).
